project / docs / model-name / YYYY-MM-DD

CONCEPT

STATUS — e.g. PROPOSAL or DRAFT

One or two sentences: the core claim or insight this deck presents. Keep under 40 words. This is the thesis, not a description.

Origin — how this model came to be
caption

The insight

Core observation. Replace this with the key insight that motivated the model.

What went wrong. Describe the failure or gap that made this necessary.

Why mechanism beats willpower. Explain why structural enforcement is required.

Synthesis. How the model resolves the tension.

§1 — Core Principle

The key claim or mechanism

One short paragraph (2-3 sentences) stating what this section proves. End with the structural insight in bold.

fig.1 — short caption describing what the diagram shows

Follow-up paragraph after the diagram. Explain the mechanism in prose. Reference specific/paths/or/commands when relevant. Key structural point in bold.

§2 — Second Axis or Detail

axis-x × axis-y — the keystone insight

Framing paragraph: what the two axes represent and why they must both be tracked. Name the prior blind spot and what tracking the second axis fixes.

fig.2 — two-axis state space; the original model only tracked one axis
axis-x = formal definition of x axis-y = formal definition of y stamped at open + close; carried in arc frontmatter condition: STALE (1) when axis-y moved but the merged delta touched no canonical section ─── the clause that fixes the original neglect ─────────────────
§3 — Components

One system — N load-bearing components

Brief claim: each component is structural. Remove one and the failure becomes possible again in that dimension.

fig.3 — system overview: N components, keystone at center
C1 Component name — short descriptor
command or definition → output details: (a) clause one (b) clause two 0 = good 1 = warn 2 = fail (FAIL-LOUD)
Enforced src/path/to/module.py — how it is enforced, where it runs. Prevents The specific failure mode this component makes impossible.
C2 Second component — short descriptor
Enforced How and where this is enforced. Prevents The failure mode this prevents.
§4 — Flow / Wiring

How the binding works end-to-end

One paragraph: the arc ↔ component ↔ output chain. State the binding key (what is carried across every surface) in bold.

Bind at birth
entry gate → side effects blocked until gate passes → owed delta = work blocked
arc ⇄ artifact ⇄ output
arc new --flag → arc frontmatter + state/arc-key + artifact body → key-a + key-b stamped
gate run as required check → independently re-derives arc⇄artifact binding from branch → fails any item whose work was not born through the gate hook refuses writes inside a scope lacking valid key. automations created with --precheck 'command' → cron firing on stale state is skipped → on debt: opens tracked issue, pushes notification Terminal: arc close → serialized merge → bumps both keys
fig.4 — enforcement is before the irreversible merge, not post-hoc
§5 — Stop Points

How this model catches the exact failure that produced it

Concrete scenario: describe the specific failure event. Then show how each component would have caught it.

  1. Component A — catches failure dimension 1
    Explain precisely how this component would have blocked or surfaced the failure. Use bold for the component name. Keep to 2-3 sentences.
  2. Component B × Component C — catches failure dimension 2
    Second stop point. Reference the specific mechanism: what trips, what surfaces it, and what the person sees.
  3. Component D — corruption is non-representable at write time
    Third stop: the corrupting state could not have been written. Even under deadline pressure, force override produces visible, payable debt.
§6 — Build Order

Fastest path to the target state

Dependency-ordered. Each step unblocks the next.

  1. Step title — component or module (C1 + C2)
    What this step does. Why it is first (smallest surface, highest leverage, unblocks everything downstream). What it delivers.
    src/path/to/module.py · src/path/to/other.py
  2. Step 2 title
    What this step delivers and why it is ordered here.
    src/path/to/file.py
  3. Step 3 title
    Step 3 description. Note what earlier step it depends on.
    src/path/to/file.py · src/path/to/other.py
CONCEPT — synthesized YYYY-MM-DD · status: PROPOSAL project / docs / concept-name
